data.body:
PASS 1 — A1 (anti-slop / over-engineering / NIH / config-surface). Findings + affirmations below. Scope: only A1; G3/G4 container/consistency findings are out of this pass's rubric.

AFFIRMATIONS (A1 passes):
- No new abstraction, dependency, or runtime config surface is introduced. The plan reuses the existing named-logger substrate (`src/rebar/_logging.py`) and the existing ruff BLE001/T201 + shrinking per-file-ignore allowlist (`pyproject.toml:178-234`, epic ring-gun-jot strangler pattern) rather than inventing anything — no NIH, no golden-hammer, no resume-driven tech.
- Resists over-engineering correctly by KEEPING the stdout `print(json.dumps(...))` machine contract instead of "cleaning it up" into the logger. Verified the real data-print sites exist and are the ones to preserve: `_commands/composer.py:247`, `_commands/show_config.py:107`, `_commands/session_log.py:187`. Migrating only stderr diagnostics is the minimal, YAGNI-correct scope.
- The scoped T201 carve-out is reuse, not proliferation: it rides the existing per-file-ignore mechanism (`pyproject.toml:230` `"src/rebar/_commands/*" = ["T201"]`), not a new config key.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] Reconcile the plan's scope against in-tree state — the codebase indicates this sweep may already be substantially DONE, which would make the plan redundant (YAGNI: no remaining current done-definition). Confirm whether fd72 is still to-do or already landed. [criteria: A1] [location: pyproject.toml:227-230; AC lines 1-4]
  evidence: `pyproject.toml:227-229` reads "CLI surface — BLE001 SWEPT by fd72 (`_cli` has no prints; removed entirely). `_commands` is the CLI presentation layer ... so it KEEPS T201; its broad-excepts were swept." In the current allowlist, `_cli/*` is ABSENT entirely and `_commands/*` = ["T201"] only (no BLE001 left). Spot-checks confirm the broad-excepts already carry justified noqas: `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py:316` (`# noqa: BLE001 — not in a repo...`), `_cli/_llm_commands.py:422` (`# noqa: BLE001 - report any failure as a clean message`), `_commands/transition.py:210` (`# noqa: BLE001 — ...fail-closed`). If this state is already in-tree, the sweep's source edits appear complete and the plan should be re-scoped to verification-only or closed.

- [ ] AC line 4 ("These packages removed from the BLE001 allowlist") has no remaining target — `_cli/*` is already absent and `_commands/*` carries only T201, so there is no BLE001 entry left to delete. Clarify the AC or mark it already-satisfied. [criteria: A1] [location: AC line 4; pyproject.toml:202-234]
  evidence: ABSENCE — across `[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]` neither `_cli` nor `_commands` carries a BLE001 ignore. The removal step is a no-op against current pyproject state.

suggested_fix (offered with moderate confidence; author to confirm the in-tree-vs-plan timing): If the BLE001 prune + except-flooring already landed, re-scope this ticket to a verification pass — "assert `make lint` + the error-handling-convention test (`tests/unit/test_error_handling_convention.py`) still pass and stdout data prints (composer/show_config/session_log) are unchanged; no source edits expected" — rather than re-doing completed work. If instead the pyproject comments are forward-looking placeholders the epic pre-wrote, keep the plan but note that so reviewers don't read it as already-done.

Note: this is a redundancy/scope COACH finding, not a blocker. The plan's intent (keep stdout data, migrate only diagnostics, reuse the existing logger + allowlist) is sound and free of over-engineering. The only load-bearing question is whether the work already exists in-tree.
